titre_probable: Fritillaries in a Copper Vase (Flowers in a Copper Vase)
description: Here's a fun one — it's a masterpiece printed on a playing card, the Five of Clubs no less! The painting shows a chubby, gleaming copper vase sitting on a table, bursting with crown imperial fritillaries. Those droopy orange-yellow blooms hang like little lanterns or ringing bells, surrounded by spiky green stems that shoot out in every direction like a botanical fireworks show. The background is a sea of electric blue dabbed with tiny dots — Van Gogh basically pixelated the wall before pixels were invented. The whole thing pops because warm orange flowers and cool blue wall are color opposites, fighting it out in the nicest way possible.
ambiance: Cheerful, energetic and buzzing with life — the flowers practically vibrate. It feels like spring caught mid-explosion, warm and joyful with that signature Van Gogh restless dancing brushwork.
